Explanation of the trends and statistics
1A) Volume and coverage of available data
column2: Generally speaking, volume and coverage increased for most data sets, due to updates and new sources. However, trends are not always revealing: in some cases, a new data set schema resulted in a lower number of records. In some other cases - e.g. oil & gas - the number of records decreased due to decreased activity

column1: Your opinion on data coverage within EMODnet for your thematic
column2: Coverage improved for most data sets. Some, such as aquaculture, remain critical and little progress was achieved. In two cases (schematic cables and submerged heritage) data had to be removed at the request of the data provider

column1: 1B) Usage of data since the start of the project phase
column2: Overall, data download increased by nearly 30%. It is not possible to measure the increase in web service usage, due to a completely different calculation method that was introduced at the beginning of the current phase. Any comparison would be inconsistent. However, recent trends seem to indicate that users are slowly transitioning to web services as opposed to data downloads. In the quarterly reports, a decreasing trend can be observed for data downlods, whereas an upward trend can be observed for web service usage
